components.definition: H is the l2-completion of sector-labeled modes |sector,k,j,q,chi>.
evidence: mode labels and corrected formal kernel are explicit

components.definition: C_fin is the finite-support span of Berger-Hopf twisted spinor modes.
evidence: finite truncations are nested subspaces of the same labeled basis

components.definition: D(D0) is the graph-norm closure of C_fin for the diagonal reference operator D0.
evidence: diagonal eigenvalue scaffold is real and lower bounded
open_obligations: prove the complete diagonal Berger/twisted Dirac spectrum and graph-norm closure

components.definition: Perturbations are defined on D(D0) if relatively bounded or bounded on the graph norm.
evidence: v1.7 relative-a estimates are below one in the scaffold
open_obligations: upgrade every finite/scaffold bound to an infinite-basis operator bound

components.definition: K_formal is the span of the three sector-labeled q=0, chi=-1 states.
evidence: the corrected formal kernel is coordinate-free and sector-labeled

components.definition: H_perp is K_formal^perp in the l2 Hilbert space.
evidence: orthogonal complement is well-defined for the finite-rank formal kernel
open_obligations: prove the full operator leaves K_formal and/or H_perp invariant or block-controlled

dense_core: C_fin = finite-support span of the infinite labeled basis
diagonal_reference_operator: D0^2 = diagonal Berger/twisted Dirac reference square
hilbert_space: H = l2({sector,k,j,q,chi}) over sector in {ell,u,d}, q=k-2j
limitations: The infinite Hilbert/domain scaffold is explicit, but graph-norm closure and perturbation domains remain conditional.
The old coordinate-first kernel (0,1,2) is not used.
title: BHSM v1.8 Infinite-Basis Domain Report
